title: Question about kaworus intro
wikitext:
[]: Question about kaworu's intro?             Quote Post by Lagann » Tue Mar 17, 2009 4:20 pm I know that in both 1.0 and the eva manga Kaworu's first appearance is simular in that he wakes up and is greeted by Keel.Did Anno base 1.0's version on the manga or was that origanally planned from the beginning and just didnt make it into the show

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Tue Mar 17, 2009 4:28 pm 1.0 and the manga are very different. In the manga he's shown ('produced' or not) in the tube being watched by Seele like Rei is when she is watched by Gendo. The actual 'greeting' doesn't matter, it's Kiel's intentions and Kaworu's wishes/duties that will make the difference. The manga is Sadamoto's own interpretation, derived from Anno's original premises. I don't think Anno would use the manga as a reference (and remember, 1.0 kaworu and manga kaworu are very different in how they are introduced), since he'll have his own ways of doing things since he drafted Evangelion in the first place (and Rebuild may be based on his reasons for reviving Evangelion). We can't really make any comparisons yet, and we can barely speculate at this point, since we don't know what Rebuild Kaworu and Seele (and Anno) are up to.

[]:             Quote Post by Lagann » Tue Mar 17, 2009 4:33 pm I could never really get into the manga because of the change in personalities,especialy Shinji and Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Tue Mar 17, 2009 4:37 pm I had this discussion somewhere before, but the manga is fantastic. In fact, it's my favorite! Just try to look at the manga as a different product of the Evangelion universe, and then you'll enjoy it. The characters are in some ways different, but their cores are still there. Things that you wouldn't have thought about with the characters are elaborated on. You really get to see another side of them (this isn't anothe way of saying they are OOC, by the way). The manga offers a lot of interesting interpretations to the Evangelion story, especially concerning the characters' psychology. I'll be killed for writing this, but really, try the manga out. It is awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Mar 17, 2009 4:44 pm I have not read the manga yet but when I do purchase it, I'll just see it as an alternate universe.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Tue Mar 17, 2009 4:46 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: I have not read the manga yet but when I do purchase it, I'll just see it as an alternate universe.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Mar 17, 2009 5:13 pm Oedipusfoot wrote: Joseph the PRPD wrote: I have not read the manga yet but when I do purchase it, I'll just see it as an alternate universe.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Mar 17, 2009 7:44 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Mar 17, 2009 7:45 pm oh dear...

[]:             Quote Post by rberlin » Tue Mar 17, 2009 7:51 pm In the 2.0 trailer, it looks like he's still naked. It makes me wonder just how much of this movie he's going to be not wearing any clothes.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Mar 17, 2009 8:09 pm Answer- NEVER. Kaworu's a nudist to make fangirls squeek.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Mar 17, 2009 8:17 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Joseph the PRPD wrote: Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by Nukie » Tue Mar 17, 2009 9:10 pm Thankfully, Kaworu (so far) looks less obnoxiously smug than EMK.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Wed Mar 18, 2009 1:45 am Nukie wrote: Thankfully, Kaworu (so far) looks less obnoxiously smug than EMK.
